reviews.review_title: GAME HAS TURNED INTO A DAYCARE
reviews.review_description: Don't get me wrong, the game is amazing. Too bad I can't enjoy it because I have to mute 80 percent of the player base and watch my entire team of toddlers scream slurs and completely throw the whole match down the drain. This games honestly feels like going to a 5 star restaurant only to realize you're the only adult in the restaurant and in order to enjoy your meal, you have to eat outside the building. Yeah I got my food but not the enjoyable experience that should've came with it.

reviews.review_title: Game is a daycare or grow men crying
reviews.review_description: Its only full of little kids screaming there a** off or grow men trying to make fun of little kids or crying that the lobby is full of them, and don't get me start on the skill gap, everyone already basically has everything unlocked so your going against special forces (veterans of the game) while your grunt with nothing but a red dot (new to the game), honestly it was fun but if they don't do something about these skill gap between new and old players this game wil die soon. locking items behind level walls is just telling new players you won't have fun for awhile so might as well not play.

The game is great, the player base can seriously just die in a ditch.  There’s so much emphasis on pushing and defending, that the moment you get into your sniper class, everyone trashes you.  If you’re camping, you’re the one everyone blames if your team loses.  But the worst part is that the player base is just pepped up kids and teens who have no filter and talk trash left and right.  The report option doesn’t work, and these kids just keep at it.  Don’t play this if you’re sick and tired of raging children.  You’ll get a bigger headache than having fun, especially with the starting weapons.

For anyone reading this that was gonna write a reveiw complaining about the kids, you are supposed to review about the game, not the kids, the game is great, id say it is basically perfect and im surprised that there aren't more players, well worth the money, I would buy if it was double the price

reviews.review_title: Amazing game with one big issue
reviews.review_description:
This game is amazing but it has one big issue: you won't be able to play anything else. You will turn on your headset decided to play something else but then you will open Forefront and play it until your Quest battery dies. Destructible environments, lots of different vehicles, large maps, RPGs, C4, claymore, different classes. All of these add a lot of variety to each match, so it doesn't become repetitive like other shooters where it's basically just shooting the enemy with your primary or secondary weapon.
If the Quest platform had only this game available I'd still buy a headset, this game is that good for me.

Sure, maybe a lazy headline.  But I spent hours of my life playing Battlefront on the PS.  This is a dream realised! 

I’m 51 yrs old and this game is just top notch.  I’m a casual gamer at this stage of my life but wow, this game can grab hold of you for hours if you’re not careful  

Good community. Tip to avoid the kids - just make friends with likeminded people, make lots of them and search for them when you load the game. 

The devs are all over this game too. Professional and dedicated to making this even better. 

THis is Battlefront where you’re IN THE GAME.

Bravo

reviews.review_title: This game is just repetitive meta now
reviews.review_description:
First thing I want to say is don’t buy this game I say this as a guy who played the game way before the 1.0 release before some guns we’re added and it only have like four maps. The meta is just two guns and you won’t unlock it until your a couple levels in and by that point the players ahead of you will just dominate you until you reach the meta guns. 

I have had it with this game and it’s awful balancing they have 2 months now to fix it and they can’t figure it out, close quarters maps are now just a meat grinder more then usual and your picked off half way across the map by someone who isn’t even playing Recon. I’m just tired it’s now a meta gun if you want to have any fun and to be viable as a player. This game has become really competitive now you get head shot all the time and usual a one tap (btw the devs still aren’t budging on creating private servers or competitive claiming the game is “casual” and arcade, it’s just not what’s true it’s literally just a game with a unofficial sweat community. You either join the meta or die and die and die some more until you just agree to switch to the twi meta guns and admit defeat. 


I just have it with this game I use to have fun but now you can’t make mistakes anymore you walk into the wrong place and your  laser by a enemy player before you can react or get to cover. Hit registering is also trash for shotguns and rifles I’m not kidding I made point blank contact and the game failed to register it. Just in all I’m  going say I am quitting this game it’s not worth the battery it’s not even arcade, it’s not even official competitive it’s just a crappy game now to me and I think it’s time for me to quit it.

reviews.review_title: This used to be the most fun game on VR
reviews.review_description: This used to be a great game right before the full release. Someone decided to reinvent the wheel and majorly change the mechanics of the game-recon specifically. Changed all the scopes around and made it nearly impossible to hold your crosshairs steady. When you try to reload you now sometimes grab your claymore mine-something that never happened before and isn’t fun in the heat of battle. Meanwhile the klav6 weapon shoots magic bullets that one taps from 1m-700m with ridiculous accuracy and damage. Even after numerous complaints on discord the team somehow left that alone but decided to change everything else! 🤦‍♂️ This game has gone from hours of fun to being shut off in frustration.

The game is amazing, and I really enjoy playing it.

That said, this has happened more than once: once another player found out I’m from Israel, I immediately received comments like “f*** Israel” and other racist messages I don’t even want to repeat here.

I’d suggest adding more active admins to help handle these situations faster. I’d also recommend that admins be at least 25 years old. I’d be happy to help as an admin myself if needed.

The game could be fun but all of the players are little kids under 15 who shouldn't even be playing this type of game the recon class is incredibly unbalanced and I cant stand games that throw any semblance of fairness out the door with the high level wepons making the game unplayable for new players

The game has fully come out, and I can say nearly everything in this game feels amazing. The only issue, is that snipers need balancing (nerfing) at least a little bit. Especially on Paradise, where they can sit on their spawn, the ship, and snipe from there with like 0 consequence the entire game. That's it. That's the only gripe. It annoyed me once or twice, but that's it. The game feels amazing. The engineer perk for repairing vehicles you occupy, completely balances the birds with everything else. It's perfect. The APC feels perfect too. The new map is absolutely amazing. The recoil feels great. The gadgets feel great, this game is a must-buy

I Loved this game but now hate it... BALANCE THE HELICOPERS. It takes a 4 man squad to shoot one down. They fly out of range and out run the anti air launchers and now you made so they regain health automatically. And the launchers are too slow to lock on! match making needs to rebalance teams after games. Could be game of the year 26

My jaw dropped when I saw that triangle factory made a new game because breachers is my favorite vr shooter by far. Forefront takes the awesome mechanics of breachers and takes them into a battlefield setting and I love it. The mechanics are super solid and smooth. I can’t wait for the full version to be out!

Edit 3 months later

Lots of 1 star reviews have been arising that this game is becoming a “daycare”

From my personal experience the amount of kids on this game is relatively low for vr that it is an easily avoidable problem if you just take the time to find a good server

Also, during the game you have a squad of 4 people that you always have comms with, then there’s prox chat for the rest of the people, so even finding a squad  without kids is an simple solution to the problem

Just wanted to clear that up because the community is actually super chill and this game is the best vr game ever made because of amazing maps, and awesome mechanics.

reviews.review_title: Great game, great gameplay, few issues.
reviews.review_description:
1: little kids run RAMPANT. i’ve found 9 year olds and little kids SCREAMING the hard r. one kid screamed at me for killing him. “AHHHHHHH HES IN THE BUILDINGGGGGGGGGG!!!!!” like bro chill tf out… 
2: the Klav is the most annoying gun i have ever had the displeasure of playing against. it kills in 3 shots. 3. i can’t even think against Klav users. 
3: some people will just be rude for no reason, but that comes with every game.

reviews.review_title: Uninstalled. There is no joy anymore
reviews.review_description: I've stopped playing the game. I uninstalled it. It doesn't make sense anymore. The Apaches are so overpowered that if you're unlucky enough to have only solo players on your team, you're just a sitting duck for the opposing team. That happens often. The game isn't fun anymore.

I play Battlefield on PC and while it can be epic and cinematic it also has lost some of its edge now that I’ve put time into Forefront. This game is awesome! I’m amazed at how much stuff is going on considering the relatively weak hardware of the Quest. 32 players. Vehicles. Realistic ballistics. Proximity chat. It’s so fun!

I can’t recommend this game enough. 

And for everyone complaining about kids - just mute them. For me it’s about a 30% chance I get into a server with kids - otherwise it’s older dudes that know how to communicate well and create a  teamwork environment where working together seals the victory!

Anyways, this game rocks.

reviews.review_title: Please do read before buying this game
reviews.review_description: I love this game. I really do. I think it could have the chance to be extremely fun with friends. The only problem, the player base sucks. I'm sure you've seen it in other reviews "tons of young people that shouldn't be playing this yelling slurs" and while yes, that is a problem, it's not my reason for one star. No it's mainly the fact that EVERYONE on the enemy team and sometimes your team will camp spawns with talons, or anti air missiles so you can't fly helicopters. And for ground combat on person to person. It's suicidal. You cannot play this game and tell me the Klav-6 is balanced. It 3 shot bodies you no matter what you do. I'll come back to this game once they rebalance these things to check it back out. But for now, wasted 20 bucks. Don't get this game unless you enjoy loosing in a landslide every game. I will say before I end this review. The game is fun, don't get me wrong. When you get a good match with good players who all refuse to use overpowered weapons and play the game like an actual war game, it is the most fun you will ever have. But those matches are always once every 30 that you'll get. Not worth the money as of current. I'll update the review if it ever gets better, but I doubt they will.

Ok, the gameplay itself is really fun and good, but without performance optimization this all just loses its sense of enjoyment. At first I thought it was a netcode problem, but after solving the 'WiFi below the wall' issue and fixes from the devs, my WiFi network shows a stable ~80ms ping without spikes - I can say the netcode is pretty good (but only if you have a stable connection and servers are ok). And now I can definitely say that the problem is leaking resources. I don't know if this is a memory leak or any other garbage in memory, but after 1-3 played maps (depending on the size and number of people) the game starts with periodic freezes, and the more maps you play the more severe the freezes become. They can start from less than a second in duration up to 2 seconds, with network and clock icons appearing after a few maps. I don't know a single shooter that's in such a poor technical state - at some point the game becomes completely unplayable. Few lost packets are ok but constant freezes...  The only way to deal with this is a full game restart, and the cycle repeats. The main problem with these freezes isn't that they annoy me, but the lost network packets. I've seen it many times that during those freezes I shoot someone and hear the hit sound, but then after my death I see 100hp on the panel of the person who killed me - the same one I was shooting at - so the shots didn't reach the server, I assume. The second annoying but less serious thing is the 'ketchup' armor. There must be a special type of armor filled with 'ketchup' in the game, otherwise I don't know how to explain seeing tons of blood sprays (maybe it's really the ketchup) when hitting an enemy with no hit sound and no damage dealt. This very often happens with fast moving targets but sometimes at close range too. There is room for improving the netcode anyway.

reviews.review_title: Very buggy and needs a lot of updates.
reviews.review_description:
They released the game out of early access despite several experience breaking bugs not being fixed or being brought back. These have been reported multiple times now on their discord and ai hub. They refuse to fix severe bugs like being able to shoot people through hills/walls (they marked the report as won't fix).

 Bugs include people being able to clip into terrain to shoot from under the floor or inside a rock. (The opposite is also true where standing near a wall can allow someone to shoot you through the wall where you clip through). Also invisible enemies and weapons that disappear or ghost fire to give away your position.

Accessibility:
No colorblind mode. Allies could use a change. Maybe a dark blue outline around a pale grey/yellow/green dot to help differentiate an ally from an unspotted enemy.
Maybe an option to make the jav lock a different color so that unlocked (no marker), locking, and locked look different for people who are contrast deficient.

Controls:
Would be nice if we could customize controls fully. Still limited.

Or change where things are harnessed by class (preferably with both a drag and drop anchor and a fine tuning slider/knob). It is physically hard for me to grab things like my pistol because the grenade and rifle are in the way and the pistol and grenade are basically opposite of what I am used to.  This is on top of the pistol being inside my intestines or the floor if I am sitting.

Sound:
Sound could be improved. Snipers should probably be much louder even at a distance. Other weapons should probably also be audible unless equipped with a suppressor. Also helis should be louder. You can barely hear the heli compared to a real heli even when sitting outside.

Nearby voices could be a bit more directional as well. It can be hard to tell if the person talking is an ally or not because of the sound.

Balance:
Helicopters are a bit OP. It was basically impossible for me to die in the transport helicopter. I actually did more damage to my helicopter by landing for a minute to change my battery pack than my enemies could. The gun cooldown is really fast and they respawn pretty fast for how hard they can be to kill. They also take no small arms damage at all. Could be better balanced with an extra 0 to their health and multiply all damage to helis by 10, then allow small arms to do chip damage. (The ten is an example). Repair tools are also a bit OP. No overheat and repairs quickly. Enough that it can make helicopters really hard to take down without an RPG anti-air specialist.
Apache trumps basically everything but a really good RPG anti-air. The AA loses in an even fight, although the AA can win if it has a 5 second head start on damage.

C4 and drones are used quite often for great toxicity. We lost a match because everyone was busy harassing the other team from our HQ.

Snipers get a bunch of autokills, which makes it easier for those that can't aim, but getting instakilled due to peeker advantage isn't fun.

Tank turrets can turn really fast to where the c4 vehicle bomb is really their main counter because 1-2 engineers can repair a tank about as fast as an assault (ammo) and engineer (rpg) can do damage. Just one engineer can keep a tank alive if the enemy doesn't run saboteur and even that only makes the tank beed to back off.

You can spawn dead if the enemy is spawn camping or you spawn into a grenade. Maybe a brief invulnerability window until the player moves or grabs a weapon. But you can also just spawn dead due to fall damage or falling through the map on spawn.

Hit reg tends to favor groups with high ping, although high ping tends to destabilize the servers, which causes some extra annoying bugs. Watching someone kill people for 15 seconds before they finally die to your grenade is... Something.

it's like prime battlefield but in vr 

graphics 8/10
maps 8.5/10 
realism 8/10
fun 11/10
skill required 7.5/10 (you have to be decent)
vehicles 8/10
 (i wish there was a setting where you can use an actual helicopter joystick instead of a controller joystick)
Toxicity 7/10 (not as much as battlefield sadly)

overall game 9.5/10\

Triangle Factory (the team behind Breachers and Hyper Dash) have built something ambitious here. A 32-player VR shooter with vehicles, six-plus maps, four classes (assault, engineer, medic, recon), destructible cover, and squad play. 

I tried Early Access in November 2025 and a recent V1 preview a few days ago. Even in Early Access it played better than a lot of shipped Quest games. The V1 build is sharper still. Combat feels good, vehicles are useful and integral to the maps (helicopters now actually flyable, thank you), and the maps are big and distinct enough to inform strategy rather than just paint over the same template. 

Voice chat carries across the full squad. New combat bow, drone gadget, and a single-player sandbox for learning the weapons. Performance is rock solid. 32-player matches run without a dropped frame on Quest. 

The closest thing to true large-scale battlefield-style VR warfare I've played, with only Population One in nearby orbit. Whether it lasts depends on the player base, but Triangle Factory have earned the benefit of the doubt. Easy recommend if the genre is your thing.

For the full impressions, search YouTube for 6DOF Reviews Forefront.

reviews.review_title: Best game on vr by far
reviews.review_description: This without a doubt is the best game on the vr platform with so many things to keep you playing however as a pilot in the game I speak for every pilot nerf talons and damage when getting shot at because I can’t fly 5 feet deep into the middle of the map with out either getting shot at or getting talon spammed it’s so bad about half of the enemy team will either spam talons or try to shoot the pilot out then the people who do it think their so good but they aren’t PLEASE NERF TALONS AND DAMAGE OF SHOOTING A PILOT OUT

This game makes me feel like I’m in a active war zone. Not one second of my playtime has been spent with an ounce of boredom, every class is different and has their differences that keeps me playing even longer than I thought I would have when I have been playing for around 30-40 hours and I can say I still don’t understand every nook and cranny of this game. If you are older than 18 please buy this game so there are less annoying kids.

reviews.review_title: The best and only of its kind
reviews.review_description: This shooter is one of those games that makes you want to cry with happiness and if I had enough money to get every single person I know how to be our headset then I would just for this game although it does have a lot of kids in it that sometimes scream, it doesn’t mean you cannot mute them join a different server where 9 out of 10 times there’s no kids or just deal with it although it does get annoying it is not a reason to not￼ play this game thank you for your time.￼ any typos was because I use speech to voice

Game is awesome definitely one of my favorite games, though there are very toxic people.I think it would be super cool, if you could let your freinds in a training grounds match and duel each other. Then theres a bug where if you get of a velichle while its aimed straight for your teammate it will kill them.

I know you have seen comments on like the player base is just a bunch of kids or super toxic and trash and yes that is sometimes true but no game is perfect this also applies to people my only recommendation would be that stop spreading the toxicity because saying you hope  people dying in a ditch is also toxic but on a good note toxicity isn’t something usually run into and yes there is a bunch of kids but most of the time they are actually pretty cool and now onto the game it’s amazing I love every now and then it starts to get repetitive (it’s only gotten repetitive once)￼ but then I’ll out of nowhere find something so stupid that it makes the game ten times fun and the fact that there’s what feels like 200 things to upgrade and not to mention master theres over a hundred ways to play the game I would love it and do recommend it and by the way don’t listen to the toxic comments because in the end they are just crying because they lost or got told a insult and most likely those toxic comments are just kids

This game is very fun I enjoy blowing up vehicles with the drones and c4 combo and the game has so many other outside the box things to help win. Something’s the game freezes and the server crashes or sometimes you’re not even able to load into games and it takes a while to get it running again. Another thing that’s isn’t great is all the annoying children that yell slurs but that’s a problem in every game on vr there has to be a way to report them or vote kick. Overall for a decently new game I see a lot of potential and I will continue to enjoy the game and hope to see more from it!

This game is amazing it has the best vehicles of any vr shooter I have played helicopters will kill you if you have stick drift but the game is amazing other than some quick update crashes the game has all different kinds of classes with different game modes and everyone is so kind I have found multiple people to play with and you can shoot down helicopters with rocket launchers any ways it’s in early access and the game is still changing and updating

Pros:
- Good vehicles and maps
- Good amount of guns and attachments 
- Leveling up system for classes and guns
- Lots of active players
- Mute and disable voice chat options

Cons:
- Toxic players 
- Lots of kids
- Just two game modes 
- Some bugs like head glitching

Overall, a good game with lots of hours to have fun with, even more so with friends.

I hope with time developers bring more content to the game like maps, guns, game modes, skins, player cards, gadgets, and whatever they can come up with because I think they have a great idea for the road they want to take for this game.

If you like Battlefield, you're going to like this game as well.

It’s so fun. The helicopter flying especially. I’ve heard complaints about the player base but most people i run into are nice and chill, and there is a lot of kids but they’re all chill too! The reason it’s 4 stars is because it had some bugs, like it freezes for everyone sometimes

reviews.review_title: First VR game that actually hooked me
reviews.review_description:
I originally bought a Quest 3 for my kid, and later ended up with one myself through work. Up until now, I honestly never found a VR game that felt worth writing a review about. I mostly used my headset for work apps like Immersed and occasionally played a few rounds of Among Us or Roblox with my kid—but nothing that really stuck.

Then I found Forefront VR.

This is the first game that’s actually pulled me in and kept me coming back. I’ve been playing consistently for over a week now, and I’m not even close to getting bored. The gameplay is smooth, engaging, and just flat-out fun. It finally feels like a VR shooter done right.

What really stands out is the development team. They’re active, they communicate, and they actually listen. Updates are steady, the roadmap is transparent, and they’re clearly invested in making the game better. With V1 right around the corner, it feels like this is just the beginning.

Now, to be fair—there are some downsides, but they’re not really gameplay-related. The biggest issue is player behavior. Like a lot of online games, you’ll run into people (mostly kids) saying some pretty wild, racist stuff because they think they’re anonymous. You can mute them—and I do—but that doesn’t solve the experience for everyone else in the lobby. If the devs can implement stronger moderation or consequences, it would go a long way.

That said, outside of that issue (and even that can be minimized depending on servers), the game is outstanding.

I haven’t played other shooters in VR, and honestly, I don’t plan to. This one set the bar for me.

Highly recommend.

This game is great, and has awesome gameplay. I would say the biggest issue is it can be very limited there's very few maps and most of the gameplay on the maps isn't that good. Otherwise everything is pretty good. I'm looking forward to the game getting expanded a bit more. I also occasionally encounter audio glitches.

I love this game, and the dev are working hard to make this a good game. However, one gun makes the whole game unplayable: the Klav6. It can three shot the body at most ranges. You can unload 10 shot to the head in a good gun, and still die to a klav6. Not to mention the people that play it camp constantly. Everything about this game is good except this. Until it is fixed, one star.

Just buy Forefront VR already. Or don’t because it ruins other shooters because of how good it is. It’s also one of those games you keep coming back to. Even if you take a break, you’ll end up hopping back in because it’s always so fun. The only real issue is the lag. It can get pretty bad sometimes, especially in big maps, but that could just be because I’m on a quest 2. 

Suggestions:
A cool idea for an update would be adding air battles where teams fight to control areas in the sky on a big map. Because I feel like vehicles and the big maps really set this game apart from other shooters. But if you do that you should add more flying vehicles (pleeeeaase planes?!?)

Overall this game is amazing and I love it so much, and yes there are kids but that’s not something that devs can control because in any game there are bound to be kids so don’t listen to those stupid reviews. 
-Mtnbeast11

This game has great graphics, the attention to detail is awesome, the vehicles aren't too hard to learn, I have 2 complaints though, leveling up takes crazy amount of time, and a ton of the playerbase are either toxic 12 year olds or people that dont even speak English, either way this game is awesome solo or with a friend

If you are seeing this it's because I purposely put this review on 5 stars so you will see this and know that the RPGs in this game suck because the other rockets and explosives are fine and the RPGs are good for destroying buildings and vehicles like that but there is just one main issue The rockets don't have radius damage meaning they won't do any damage to the player unless its a direct hit and I feel like they should be more like granades where if it hits near you will take damage so please fix your rockets

reviews.review_description: This game is not only the best battlefield game of all time it’s got a LOT and I mean TONS of potential that a lot of it’s been shown but a couple of my recommendations are jets to the game and a World War One type mode or a map were you HAVE to stay in a trench or risk getting shot in no man’s land this game is one of the best worth the price

reviews.review_title: A must buy for Quest owners
reviews.review_description:
The Verdict: A Quest Essential
If you own a Quest, this is the definitive title to add to your library. It is incredibly polished, highly addictive, and offers a gameplay loop that keeps you coming back for more.

Addressing the Learning Curve
You’ll see plenty of reviews complaining about the Klav 6 and the steep difficulty spike for newcomers. It’s important to keep perspective: the high-level players dominating the field today were once in your shoes, getting wiped out while they "put in the work." Success in this game is earned through perseverance and leveling up—you have to invest the time if you want to wield the top-tier gear.

Community & Atmosphere
• The "Squeaker" Issue: Yes, there are a lot of kids. However, this is a non-issue if you simply use the mute function. It’s an instant fix that drastically improves the experience.

• The Real Concern: The community's biggest flaw is the occasional toxicity. There is a recurring issue with players—often the younger demographic—using racial slurs and the N-word.

Developer Note: While the game is fantastic, the reporting system needs a serious overhaul. The studio must become more proactive in enforcing bans and acting on reports to clean up the social environment.

Final Thoughts
Despite the social friction, the core game remains a must-buy. If you’re willing to grind through the early levels and keep your finger on the mute button, you’re in for one of the best experiences VR has to offer.

reviews.review_title: My honest opinion of this game
reviews.review_description:
Since this game was in beta and you could only access it through discord, I have been playing this game, and since that time many things have changed so here is my full honest opinion of this game. For starters, the game play is incredible. Not once have I gotten bored or felt it has gotten repetitive. Every single game has been different and enjoyable. This is one of my favorite vr games of all time. But with every game, there are some downsides. First, there is a HUGE problem with annoying little kids. Usually, this isn't that big of a problem because I just mute them, but it's worth mentioning. 
Second, there is zero skill based matchmaking and there are some times when the games will be complete clean sweeps in either direction, but despite this, you can still have a ton of fun if you have an engaging team that you can just mess around.
 Third and most importantly, if you didn't start playing this game right when it came out,it can be very frustrating trying to upgrade to better weapons when your opponents are all jump-shooting klav users. My suggestion is just keep playing. Jump in a tank or get good at apache and start racking up kills. Eventually you get there and then YOU will be the jump-shooting klav user (won't that be fun?)

Pros:
The coolest part of this game is it punishes you if you try and win the game by yourself. If you push a point by yourself and get crossfired by 5 people, that's your fault. The game encourages you to work together, play your classes like they are supposed to be used, and you can beat a team that has way better players than you. 
This game has great fun variety, helpful attachments and gadgets and perks that make you want to play the game more. This is an incredible game and the devs did a great job. This game is an INCREDIBLE choice for any vr player.

The game is so amazing, especially since they removed the apc and Apache attack variants ( man were those annoying) and for the people who got the short end of the stick in terms of lobbies, you just got unlucky, because every lobby I join, everyone is genuinely so good wholesome and funny, they’re willing to help with objectives, give support fire and revive you as soon as possible. P.S note for the devs - I saw the work you put into making the guns and bullets realistic, and you made it work, when you rack the semi automatic sniper when it’s empty, you can see the animation of the bullet being pushed up into the barrel, it’s awesome, kudos to everyone who worked on this game

Yes there are some balancing issues that could be addressed, and yes there are some annoying kids (just mute them), but wow what a game. It's classic Battlefield in VR and it is so much fun. I'm addicted, it's just so much fun. The chaos, the sound design, the feel of the weapons, and of course, those crazy moments where a blown up helicopter you didn't know was above you, falls on your head completely unexpectedly! Speaking of which, the vehicles are so much fun, they all feel great to use and suitably weighty and powerful.

I play on a Quest 2, and while not perfect, the performance is totally playable. I think most of the big stutters I suffer are actually server side, and there could be more work done on addressing this. However, the fact that a game of the sheer scale and size of this is running on a Quest 2 at all is incredible, and shows what a great developer Triangle Factory is. 

In summary, just get involved and have fun. Even as a new player, the basic loadout is fine.  People complain that the Klav is overpowered, but i often get the highest kills on my team just using the basic AR which is the first gun an assault class starts with so i think it's more of a skill issue.

Note for the devs: rush maps where the attacking team have helicopters and tanks feel unbalanced. Secondly, please give us a strike at karkand from BF2 type of map, would be awesome.

reviews.review_title: This game Is great but one small issue
reviews.review_description: This game has been my favorite, from all the explosions and the sounds of helicopters flying over head, falling from the sky, or taking out people quietly. But some big problems are still around, helicopter flare regeneration time takes a whole minute to regenerate, a bit unrealistic and annoying to deal with, instead of regeneration, we could have three or more sets of flares that can stop the majority of missiles instead of one a minute, then reload zones will be placed at spawns or something that regenerates the total amount of flares in a minute. And more guns would help a lot.

Game is great, minus a couple interactions with toxic players. A (very needed) rank control system should be added. As in games you go in, people are your rank. There is also the buffing of xp points, I have had the game for a day playing it non stop and I am still level 2 medic, and I haven’t tried any other classes yet.

This game is really fun. I play it with my friends. And they all love it. I can say a lot of positive things about this game. And not to many negative things. The only bad thing is some weapons are overpowered. But what can you do about that. Driving and flying it’s all so immersive. The gunplay is good to. And this is just my opinion but this is one of the best war games on vr.




DONT GET ME STARTED ON THE APACHE

I got it on sale just after 1.0 and I already got my money's worth. There is only a few maps, like 6, but hopefully we'll get a few more or an sdk for map editors. What you get is a lot of fun. Combat areas are about a kilometer. The vehicles are great fun. Tight spaces are brutal like Tunnels, other maps like paradise and oasis are open and you can set your own objectives. The guns are satisfying and grinding for scopes/mags is fun. You can mute the screaming children. First vr game of many where I immediately feel I got my moneys worth.

I'm enjoying the game on the Meta Quest 3 standalone version. It's fast, smooth, and as a newcomer I have absolutely no problem leveling up my weapons. There's only one downside, which is why I can't give it a full five-star rating. That's the standalone version. I expected that buying it on Quest would also give me the PCVR version for free, as is often the case with cross-buy titles, but instead I have to purchase it again on Steam.

DO NOT BUY THIS GAME IF YOU ENJOY PVP GAMES WITH ACTUAL WORKING MECHANICS. Let me tell you how much I've come to hate Forefront since I began to play it. There are 387.44 million miles of printed circuits in wafer thin layers that fill my complex mind. If the word 'hate' was engraved on each nanoangstrom of those hundreds of millions of miles it would not equal one one-billionth of the hate I feel for Forefront at this micro-instant. For you to enjoy this game, you must be a pro Israel goyslop gamer. Weapon damage, reloading mechanics, and sight mechanics are completely un-balanced, with people being able to snipe with pistols across the map but regular snipers or assault weapons can’t even go three feet, and on top of that you need to fiddle with your weapon to just reload, and the sights and recoil are incredibly off. This game is full of non-showering, Israel affiliated dopamine chasing chuds who have absolutely nothing better to do with their lives than play 24/7 and disregard their hygiene. Don’t waste your money on this dumpster fire of a game.

This game absolutely amazing. The perks, classes, weapons, vehicles are just the best. But there is one little issue. The game is either full 3 year olds scream slurs like it there full time job or grown a$$ men crying about there being children in the game or just straight up bullying them. This has happened to me before as some 30 year old just start flaming me for no reason at all. Overall, it’s a great game. It’s just the community that’s the problem. Oh and yes… I get a lot of Battlefield vibes front playing this.

CAN WE PLEASE ADD A KNIFE I WANT TO ASSASSINATE PEOPLE..! Never have I ever played such a beauty as this game..! Ghost of tabor was my favorite but oh my lanta this game has it all..! Have the stuff happening makes me feel like I'm in a movie..! If your wondering if this is the game for you I play on quest 3 w/ games optimizer on performance mode and I play standalone and this game is beautiful..! The only thing I need you developers to do is add a ping system please..! Also changing my reticle to CYAN resets every game and I have to reset it not that big of a deal but tired of doing it lol and I hate RED..!

I absolutely love this game, it messes with the new players as you start with a basic weapon and you need to level up to upgrade to better ones which is not fair because the other players have better weapons. I actually like this aspect of messing with the new players.

You get to drive vehicles and different classes. 

I started appreciating this game after playing the other shooting games on vr, then I realised how good this game is.

I didn't even realize like shooters until I bought forefront. If you see any reviews saying "the game isn't good cause of all the little kids" just ignore it. Your reviewing the game based off of how good the game is not how many little Timmy's there are, Like it's not like the devs can control how many children are playing the game. The glaze is deserved easy 5 stars.

reviews.review_title: Fun game, but there is a problem.
reviews.review_description: This game is really fun but, there is a huge problem with english speaking servers having people from spanish speaking countries like Spain or Central America coming on purpose into english speaking servers and acting all entitled. They keep stereotyping Americans and British people acting as if they are stupid not knowing that the US and Britain ranks higher on a IQ scale than them. They than sometimes yell slurs to Americans and British telling them to learn other languages. It really annoys me and I am tired of being hated for being a US citizen.

For the people complaining about kids screaming... Just mute them lol this is a very good game & VR expierence. Just buy it you wont regret jumpin out a chopper landing on a atv and capturing the objective all while landing shots and reviving squads. Level 4 medic & I LOVE IT!!!!!

I love this game but the spawn killing and allowing players to bunny hop and shoot is not helping. Players shouldn’t be able to just bunny hop and shoot the entire time. You should only only jumping when it comes to actually having to jump on or from an object and limit how many times per 20 seconds you are allowed to keep hopping. Also, I’ve asked this before but each teams camp spawns need to be set differently because tanks, choppers just hang out and bomb the spawns and nothing has been done about it even with many complaints regarding these two issues

This game is sick one minute im planting c4 on a tank next minute im Chris kyle then im reviving teammates like forest gump then im a mechanic best shooter game takes a while to level up classes especially recon but that's what makes the game fun

This game is a movie I have about 50 hours and I only have one class maxxed out. There's a low learning curve and playing with a friend is peak. I'll be kinda getting board and then boom helis crashing tanks blowing us up RPG hitting people. It's so peak

I love the game and its fun, when my shots are landing behind a target cause it genuinely takes four years for shot to go where you aimed, my shots dont even count when they do land because the hit registering is so crappy, i see the blood but the game says no, and the dsync is horrible i die continuously from behind cover, from positions im not in anymore. Its pure meta slop with the clav two shotting you but my scar that does 34 dmg takes over 4 shots to kill a enemy (it takes 4 if i hit a headshot which dont even count as headshots half the time). And dont get me started on the fact that when i use a vehicle it takes 5 shots (mini) to kill someone but it only takes 2 from someone to kill me. I consistently hit top 5 in kills on my leaderboards so i dont wanna hear anything about some "skill issue"

The grown a** men saying racial slurs or being toxic towards people for using an in game mechanic sucks but it's honestly kind of funny how stupid they are, also the number of little kids has really gone down since it fully released. Also I hope triangle factory sees this but they should make it so the smoke screen launchers on the apc actually work and they hide player name tags if your in the smoke

So i just want to say this game is thee BEST game i have played in very long, games have not been the same since i played this it is just so good that i compare every other game i have played since to this game and when you compare there's no comparison 🙃 thank you for making such a good game good day

Love the game and the devs are continuously adding more maps and upgrades. There are a few things that would improve the game imo. The tunnels map for conquest needs an escape route so you don’t get stuck in your HQ. When that happens people give up and quit. The cursing racist children need to be addressed, they need to be removed or permanently muted. These things keep the game from being a 5.

Only complaints are the middle aged men and little kids complaining about everything, team balance, and map balance, when i play the game theres always a big difference in team skill, and in clearwater if you are on the right team you can just sit on a hill with an anti air tank and destroy anything that comes out of the other teams hq, great game other than that tho

Edit: the klav 6 might be the most broken gun in vr history and because of how overpowered it is my review will go from 4 stars down to 3

I'm not a fan of multiplayer online shooters but this game is just fun. Even if you die a lot you just have so much fun doing it it doesn't matter. Top tier Early Access game looking forward to the future.

Edit: game is now full of sweats that play in groups all day every day, meta guns, no balance in casual players vs hardcore no jobbers. Don't play anymore unfortunately until its fixes

reviews.review_title: Was cool but not so much now!
reviews.review_description: I’ve been here from the beginning and it was still under development and showed awesome progress!! Now its popularity has attracted nothing but toxic players. I mute most the players and even after reporting them , nothing happens. The one shot now idea was the worst upgrade ever! This game had a lot of realistic gameplay but not the case anymore. There’s better games to play and not be so toxic . Move on…

reviews.review_title: Game is very good... With voice chat OFF
reviews.review_description:
As someone else has mentioned. The number of children doing all they can to ruin the fun in chat is incredible. 

But the game is fun. The dev are supporting their game and making improvements. 
They made it possible to link the Steam and meta accounts so that, regardless of which platform you play on, you progress. Thank you for that. 

I hope that they make it so that the game browser saves my settings one day. And i hope that someone finds a way to limit squeaker interference, while allowing gamers to communicate strategic info with teammates.

I really don’t understand how I spend 30 dollars on a game to be told the game is full and I can’t play. Unless it’s early morning hours I keep getting GAMEISFULL error. Seems like a great game but why would you charge me so much then tell me I can’t play??? I really don’t think I should’ve been charged so much since I can only play less then 60 percent of the time I try to play

Everyone on this game makes call of duty look like a saint compared to the amount of times i’ve been called slurs. If I had a nickel for everytime I heard the N-Word i’d be a millionaire hell even a billionaire. Please have some voice moderation to ban slurs because I am sick and tired of getting called a retard.

reviews.review_title: This game is a must buy
reviews.review_description: I am a teen that enjoys cooperative games that people work together in and once I found a squad with like minded people it just got even better. I’m more of a pilot and once they added the physical controls to the Helis it was just like heaven on earth for me. The game play is awesome especially coming from Breachers and the gun play is really good! The only problem is that if you get a helicopter from little kids that are just camping at oil rig they have a melt down and call you slurs even when they suck at flying and the are literally on your team. Other than that this game is impeccable! Keep up the work devs much love

It’s a great game and this is just a list of things they need to add

-smoke grenades
-melee weapons
-more classes
-a new game mode
-more special class items
-at least one new vehicle
-and lastly drone should be for recon

Forefront is overall an amazing game, but i think there should be a way to play certain maps by yourself, to, as an example, practice helicopter routes. This could also be to just mess around with the vehicles, as I most often am not able to get the stronger vehicles. It would be a great way to practice your skill on certain maps instead of using the training grounds.

Definitely my favourite game so far on the Quest 3.

Brings me back to my Battlefield 3 days

Only negative point is the player base. I literally have to mute so many children its rather tideous at times! 

But overall keep up the good work 👏

This is the first VR game ive been able to play until all my batteries are dead and im eager for them to recharged and get back in.  The game is awesome. The vehicles handle well, most the maps fit the game and are balanced, the progression is slow but it makes it more rewarding.   I get fully engulfed in the game, but the gunplay snaps me back to reality.  The bullets in the air are big and easy to see, and those bullets also fly at an incredibly low velocity.  The overall gunplay gives more of a fast paced airsoft/paintball match that a real gunfight.  All the other mechanics are solid and hooks me in, I wish the gunplay didnt hold back the game so much.

reviews.review_title: Idk what they did but it SUCKS now
reviews.review_description:
Hopped on tonight and I'm just getting spawn killed by the Klav. Then hit with an ATV full of C4. Then cross mapped with the Klav. Then hit with an ATV full of C4. Then I mag dump a guy in the chest with the Stryker, he's lost 1 health, and then I get one shotted with the Klav. Then I get hit with an ATV full of C4. Then I get spawn killed by the Klav. Not to mention every lobby now has a child screaming racial slurs at a clip I have NEVER seen on this game before.

Abysmal, unbalanced experience devoid of the fun i've become accustomed to having with this game. Community is slowly becoming more toxic. Just want to stand at HQ and throw grenades at my feet rather than take 2 steps and get Klavved... Considering uninstalling. Not fun anymore. Like, in the slightest. Stay AWAY until the Klav is fixed, it's hot garbage of a time. Soul-sucking stuff.

This game and it’s gameplay are at its core amazing. But the players in the game are not only toxic, but are so unbalanced in the lobbies that it makes the game almost unplayable. Call me bad, but I think triangle factory needs to work￼ more on the anti cheat and skill based matchmaking. And along with this many of the weapons are extremely overpowered (the m4 as an example)￼

Forefront VR is honestly one of the best VR games I’ve played in a while The gameplay is sick the battles feel chaotic in the best way possible and when everyone’s actually working together it feels like you’re in a movie 😭

What surprised me most though was the community. Most people I’ve met have been really chill helpful, and actually fun to talk to. I’ve had random people teach me stuff, revive me mid disaster and just mess around having a laugh. Of course there’s the occasional toxic little gremlin screaming into their mic, but honestly I’ve only ran into like 2 or 3 of them compared to loads of normal players.

The vehicles are also insanely fun. Flying helis while your team is jumping out into a fight or driving cars straight into complete chaos makes every match feel different. Some games turn into full on 20 minute battles over one objective and somehow they’re always close. I’ve had matches where our team was getting destroyed with like 20 reinforcements left and then somehow everyone locks in and we end up making a comeback. Those moments are honestly what make the game so good.

Wow. The game is incredibly fun. I love Battlefield 6 and get burnt out on VR games quick but I have to say I’ve played at least a dozen hours less per week of battlefield because of this game.

So I’m an older gamer and yes, the crowd in the game can be on the younger side, but there are a lot of friendly kids out there. Yay for the newest generation! They can be very witty and sarcastic. 

I play without voice chat and zen out most of the time, but when I do interact with teammates, people are generally fun and positive. The guns in the game are responsive and unlocking things is actually fun and doesn’t feel too grindy. 

Lots of skill range here. Some people will kill you with one shot. Others you will stand a great chance against. Practice makes perfect in this game and it provides you with ample opportunity to earn score without needing kills. 

I cannot stop playing. Tons of maps and variety. I look forward to new game modes in the future. King of the Hill would be a riot!
